IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: TO PROTECT AND EXPAND ACCESS TO SEXUAL AND REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH CARE AND RIGHTS THROUGH EDUCATION, ADVOCACY, AND LIMITED ELECTORAL ACTIVITIES.
IRS990.Desc: ADVOCACY - PROGRAMS DESIGNED TO EMPOWER ALL PEOPLE TO BUILD THE FUTURE THEY WANT AND CHANGE CULTURAL ATTITUDES ABOUT REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH.

OrganizationBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Planned Parenthood NJ Super PAC Inc
Democratic Party of South Carolina - State Account

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule C, Part I-A, Line 1 Description of Political Activities
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Planned Parenthood Action Fund, Inc. (the "Action Fund") communicated with the general public about state elections and its members and the general public about federal and state elections. Where permitted by law, its specific activities included use of the mail, internet, radio, television advertising, and phone banking to educate its members and the public about the positions of candidates on issues pertinent to reproductive health and to urge them to vote for candidates likely to advance the organization's mission. The Action Fund supported other organizations undertaking similar efforts, including: (1) fundraising and administrative expenses of a related 527 organization (Planned Parenthood Action Fund, Inc. PAC) and (2) shared facilities, supplies and administrative services for a related 527 organization, Planned Parenthood Votes.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule D, Part X, Line 1 Due to PPFA/ INTERCOMPANY
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Due to Planned Parenthood Federation of America, Inc. The amount has been reimbursed pursuant to resource sharing agreement.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule D, Part X, Line 2 FIN 48 (ASC 740) footnote
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The FIN 48 footnote per the audited financial statements states that the Action Fund recognizes the effect of income tax positions only if those positions are more likely than not of being sustained. The Action Fund believes it has taken no significant uncertain tax positions.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule D, Part XII, Line 2(d) Other expenses in audited financial statements not in form 990
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: LOSS ON PLEDGE RECEIVABLE - 200000

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule G, Part I, Line 2b(v) and FORM 990, PART IX, LINE 24A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: IN ADDITION TO PROFESSIONAL FUNDRAISER EXPENSES INCLUDED IN LINE 11E, $164,828 OF OTHER REIMBURSED EXPENSES WERE PAID DIRECTLY TO PROFESSIONAL FUNDRAISERS FOR POSTAGE, MAILHOUSE COSTS, AND MERGE/PURGE. THE PROFESSIONAL FUNDRAISER CONTRACTS AND THE INVOICES PAID DISTINGUISH BETWEEN PAYMENT FOR SERVICES AND PAYMENT FOR THESE EXPENSES. PART 1, LINE 2B, COLUMN (V) AMOUNTS PAID TO CERTAIN FUNDRAISERS RESULTED IN A CURRENT YEAR LOSS BUT SECURED FUTURE DONORS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule I, Part I, Line 2 Procedures for monitoring use of grant funds.
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION'S MANAGEMENT MONITORS ON A CONTINUING BASIS, THE USAGE OF GRANTS TO ENSURE SUCH GRANTS ARE USED FOR INTENDED PURPOSES. GRANTEES ARE REQUIRED TO SUBMIT A NARRATIVE AND FINANCIAL REPORT EXPLAINING HOW THE GRANT FUNDS WERE EXPENDED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule J, Part I, Line 3 Arrangement used to establish the top management official's compensation
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Planned Parenthood Federation of America, Inc., used the following methods to establish the compensation of the organization's president: compensation committee; independent compensation consultant; Form 990 of other organizations; compensation survey/study; and approval by the board or compensation committee.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule M, Part I Explanations of reporting method for number of contributions
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Securities - Publicly traded - Number of contributors

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part III, Line 1 DESCRIPTION OF ORGANIZATION MISSION
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ACTION FUND IS FORMED EXCLUSIVELY FOR THE PROMOTION OF SOCIAL WELFARE WITHIN THE MEANING OF SECTION 501(C)(4) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE OF 1986 AS AMENDED, AND ITS PURPOSES SHALL BE: -TO ENCOURAGE AND PROTECT INFORMED INDIVIDUAL CHOICE REGARDING REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH CARE; -TO ADVOCATE PUBLIC POLICIES WHICH GUARANTEE THE RIGHT, AS WELL AS FULL AND NONDISCRIMINATORY ACCESS, TO SUCH CARE; AND -TO FOSTER AND PRESERVE A SOCIAL AND POLITICAL CLIMATE FAVORABLE TO THE NONDISCRIMINATORY ACCESS, TO SUCH CARE; AND EXERCISE OF REPRODUCTIVE CHOICE. ALL ACTIVITIES UNDERTAKEN BY THE ACTION FUND SHALL BE IN FURTHERANCE OF, AND IN AGREEMENT WITH, THE MISSION OF PLANNED PARENTHOOD FEDERATION OF AMERICA, INC. ("PPFA"). THE ACTION FUND SHALL NOT PURSUE ANY EFFORTS THAT ARE INCONSISTENT WITH THE POLICY POSITIONS OF PPFA.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part V, Line 2a COST-SHARING AGREEMENT
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: PURSUANT TO A RESOURCE-SHARING AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE ACTION FUND AND PLANNED PARENTHOOD FEDERATION OF AMERICA - A RELATED ORGANIZATION - THE ACTION FUND IS PERMITTED TO USE PPFA EMPLOYEES TO CONDUCT ACTION FUND ACTIVITIES AND REIMBURSES PPFA FOR ALL COSTS RELATED TO SUCH USE. PPFA ISSUES THE RELEVANT IRS FORMS AND OTHERWISE UNDERTAKES COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS FOR ITS EMPLOYEES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 15 COMPENSATION REVIEW PROCESS
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Pursuant to a resource-sharing agreement between the Action Fund and PPFA - a related organization - the Action Fund is permitted to use PPFA employees to conduct Action Fund activities so long as the Action Fund reimburses PPFA the allocable share of the cost of such use. PPFA has a compensation setting body (the "Body") that reviews and approves the compensation of the leadership staff of PPFA, including the president and CEO, chief financial officer, EVP and chief operating officer, and other members of the executive team. This independent Body is comprised of the officers of the PPFA board and 3 other directors, with the chair of the board serving as its chair. The review and approval of the salaries of these employees takes place on an annual basis using comparability data such as industry surveys, documented compensation of persons holding similar positions in similar organizations, and/or independent compensation studies. The proceedings are documented contemporaneously in minutes. The most recent review of the compensation of the President and CEO occurred in November 2024.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 1a Delegate broad authority to a committee
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ACTION FUND HAS AN EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE THAT MAY EXERCISE ALL THE AUTHORITY OF THE FULL BOARD EXCEPT FOR: MATTERS REQUIRING THE APPROVAL OF THE REGULAR MEMBERS; FILLING OF VACANCIES ON THE BOARD OR COMMITTEES; AMENDMENT OR REPEAL OF RESOLUTIONS OF THE BOARD THAT BY THEIR TERMS MAY NOT BE AMENDED OR REPEALED BY THE COMMITTEE; REMOVAL OF VOTING DIRECTORS; INDEMNIFICATION; DISPOSITION OF REAL PROPERTY; AND DISSOLUTION. ALL MEMBERS OF THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE ARE MEMBERS OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 2 Family/business relationships amongst interested persons
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Alexis McGill-Johnson, Ilana Esterrich, Jacqueline Ayers, Susan Manning, Vickie Barrow-Klein - Business relationship

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 6 Classes of members or stockholders
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ACTION FUND IS A NOT-FOR-PROFIT MEMBERSHIP ORGANIZATION. THERE ARE THREE CLASSES OF MEMBERS OF THE ACTION FUND: REGULAR, CONTRIBUTING AND ASSOCIATE MEMBERS. THE ASSOCIATE MEMBERS ELECT ONE DIRECTOR; THE REGULAR MEMBERS ELECT THE BALANCE OF THE DIRECTORS. REGULAR MEMBERS ARE THOSE INDIVIDUALS WHO SERVE AS VOTING MEMBERS OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF PPFA. CONTRIBUTING MEMBERS ARE THOSE INDIVIDUALS WHO: (1) PAY ANNUAL DUES IN AN AMOUNT ESTABLISHED BY THE BOARD; OR (2) ARE "LIFETIME MEMBERS" AS A RESULT OF MAKING ONE OR MORE DUES PAYMENTS IN AN AMOUNT ESTABLISHED BY THE BOARD. CONTRIBUTING MEMBERS HAVE NO VOTING OR OTHER RIGHTS WITH RESPECT TO THE CORPORATION. ASSOCIATE MEMBERS ARE THOSE INDIVIDUALS WHO ANNUALLY AFFIRM THEIR DESIRE TO BE A MEMBER PURSUANT TO A PROPERLY EXECUTED FORM PROVIDED BY THE CORPORATION. ASSOCIATE MEMBERS HAVE NO VOTING RIGHTS OR OTHER RIGHTS WITH RESPECT TO THE CORPORATION, EXCEPT THAT THEY MAY PARTICIPATE IN THE PLANNED PARENTHOOD ACTION FUND, INC. ELECTION OF ONE VOTING MEMBER OF THE BOARD, THE ASSOCIATE DIRECTOR.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 7a Members or stockholders electing members of governing body
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: SEE RESPONSE TO PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 6.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 7b Decisions requiring approval by members or stockholders
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: IN ADDITION TO THE BOARD, THE REGULAR MEMBERS APPROVE CHANGES TO THE BYLAWS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 11b Review of form 990 by governing body
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ACTION FUND'S FORM 990 IS PREPARED BY AN INDEPENDENT PAID TAX PREPARER. THE DRAFT RETURN IS REVIEWED BY THE ORGANIZATION'S SHARED FINANCE STAFF, CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER AND THE LEGAL DEPARTMENT. THE FINAL DRAFT 990 IS REVIEWED BY THE ACTION FUND'S FINANCE COMMITTEE. ONCE THE DRAFT FORM 990 IS APPROVED BY THE FINANCE COMMITTEE, COPIES OF THE COMPLETE FORM 990 ARE PROVIDED TO EACH VOTING MEMBER OF THE GOVERNING BOARD PRIOR TO SUBMISSION AND FILING WITH THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 12c Conflict of interest policy
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: PURSUANT TO A RESOURCE-SHARING AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE ACTION FUND AND PPFA - A RELATED ORGANIZATION - THE ACTION FUND IS PERMITTED TO USE PPFA EMPLOYEES TO CONDUCT ACTION FUND ACTIVITIES. ANNUALLY, PPFA ASKS ITS KEY EMPLOYEES, OTHER EMPLOYEES, AND OFFICERS TO REVIEW AND SIGN A CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY. IN ADDITION, THE ACTION FUND HAS ITS BOARD MEMBERS SIGN A CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY ANNUALLY. THE ACTION FUND'S LEGAL COUNSEL FOLLOWS UP TO RESOLVE ANY DISCLOSED CONFLICTS. IF A CONFLICT IS IDENTIFIED, THE INTERESTED INDIVIDUAL MAY NOT BE PRESENT AT, OR PARTICIPATE IN DELIBERATION, OR VOTE ON THE MATTER GIVING RISE TO THE CONFLICT.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 19 Required documents available to the public
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ACTION FUND'S FINANCIAL REPORT AND FORM 990 ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part IX, Line 11g Other Fees
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Consultant Fees - Total Expense: 5626134, Program Service Expense: 5198057, Management and General Expenses: 428077, Fundraising Expenses: ;

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part XI, Line 9 Other changes in net assets or fund balances
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: LOSS ON PLEDGE RECEIVABLE - -200000;

DisregardedEntityName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: PLANNED PARENTHOOD ACTION FUND INC PAC
